topic: Browser-side automation for harvesting Ancestry.com cousin trees
tos_warning: Ancestry ToS (eff. 2025-08-18) explicitly prohibits bulk/automated data collection. Low-rate personal use with browser extensions is a grey zone with low ban risk in practice.

github_repos.name: patchright
github_repos.use_case: Undetected Playwright — patches webdriver detection, sandboxes page agent JS, bypasses Akamai fingerprinting. Best for automating Ancestry tree navigation.
github_repos.install: pip install patchright && patchright install chromium

github_repos.name: getmyancestors
github_repos.use_case: GEDCOM exporter for FamilySearch. Useful only if cousin has a linked FamilySearch tree.
github_repos.note: Does not work with Ancestry.com directly

github_repos.name: ancestryDnaWrapper
github_repos.use_case: Python wrapper for Ancestry's undocumented DNA REST API. Cookie-based auth via browser_cookie3. Documents DNA match/test endpoints. Does NOT cover tree/person data.

github_repos.name: 23andMeFamilyTreeScraper
github_repos.use_case: Pattern reference only — not Ancestry. Shows technique for cookie-auth tree scraping.

github_repos.name: genscrape
github_repos.use_case: JS library for DOM scraping of Ancestry person pages. Last release 2018. Ancestry DOM has changed — likely broken.

github_repos.name: ancestry-scraper
github_repos.use_case: Old Selenium bot. Ancestry login flow completely changed. Do not use.

chrome_extensions.name: One2Tree
setup: Navigate to cousin's public tree on Ancestry
Set max generation depth, press Go
Download GEDCOM from the results tab
chrome_extensions.recommendation: BEST FREE/LOW-COST OPTION for bulk GEDCOM extraction

chrome_extensions.name: Pedigree Thief
chrome_extensions.ancestry_support: NO — Ancestry support was broken when Ancestry blocked third-party tools in 2020.

chrome_extensions.name: Genea Ancestry Add To Tree Auto-Clicker
chrome_extensions.status: UNCERTAIN — listing returned blank page, may be delisted or broken
chrome_extensions.note: Verify manually before relying on this. Do not install without reviewing source.

chrome_extensions.name: Ancestry Media Download
chrome_extensions.use_case: Media/photo downloads only — not tree data

userscripts.assessment: Extremely thin catalogue. No bulk tree export or save-to-tree automation script exists publicly as of April 2026.

scripts.name: Script_Ancestry.com_Hint_Helper
scripts.what_it_does: Assists hint merge page with data validation and best-practice enforcement. Not a bulk auto-accept tool.

scripts.name: Ancestry.com - Remove paid hints
scripts.what_it_does: Hides paid-tier hints. Not relevant to tree harvesting.

dnagedcom.ancestry_status: BROKEN — Ancestry blocked third-party match downloads in 2020. Client's Ancestry function is non-functional as of 2024+.
dnagedcom.tree_download: NO — DNAGedcom downloads DNA match metadata, not tree/GEDCOM data.
dnagedcom.verdict: Not useful for tree harvesting from Ancestry. Useful for FamilyTreeDNA/23andMe DNA match work.

ancestry_api_endpoints.note: Undocumented, reverse-engineered from community tools and DevTools observation. May change without notice.

tree_endpoints.note: Observed in DevTools Network tab. Many return 403 for other users' trees despite being 'public'.

devtools_approach.description: Manual capture of JSON responses via Chrome DevTools Network tab and HAR export
steps: 1. Log in to Ancestry. Navigate to cousin's public tree.
2. F12 > Network tab > check Preserve log > filter Fetch/XHR.
3. Click through the tree (each person click triggers JSON fetches).
4. Identify requests to api.ancestry.com or www.ancestry.com/api/ returning JSON.
5. Right-click > Copy response for each, OR: Network tab > right-click any request > Save all as HAR.
6. Parse HAR/JSON with Python to extract person records and emit GEDCOM.
devtools_approach.limitation: Lazy-loading means you must manually trigger loads for all generations by clicking. Not fully automated.
devtools_approach.output: raw JSON, converted to GEDCOM via script

akamai_detection_methods: Behavioral: no mouse movement, instant clicks, no scroll
Cookies: _abck and bm_sz set by Akamai for session tracking

tools.name: patchright
tools.install: pip install patchright && patchright install chromium
key_patches: Sandboxes Playwright page agent JS (invisible to page scripts)
tools.session_cookie_trick: Export cookies from logged-in Chrome session, load into patchright context via context.add_cookies(). Bypasses login detection.

tools.name: camoufox
tools.status: UNRELIABLE in April 2026 — maintainer reports year-long gap in development, performance degraded
tools.verdict: Skip for now. Revisit in 6+ months.

tools.name: curl-cffi
tools.use_case: Direct API calls with real browser TLS fingerprint. Lighter than full browser automation.
tools.limitation: No JavaScript execution — cannot handle Ancestry's JS challenge. Works only for pure API endpoints.

best_practice: Export session cookies from a manual login in real Chrome — do not automate the login itself.
Load cookies into the automation context to skip login.
Add human-like delays: random.uniform(1.5, 4.0) seconds between page loads.
Add mouse movement simulation: page.mouse.move() between actions.
Do not make more than 1 tree page request per 3-5 seconds.
Run during off-peak hours to reduce rate-limit exposure.

ranking.verdict: BEST for 20-30 trees. Actively maintained, low detection risk, native GEDCOM export.

ranking.method: DevTools HAR capture + Python GEDCOM parser
ranking.verdict: Best fully-free option. Requires clicking through each tree but captures all JSON.

ranking.speed: automated but rate-limited to ~3-5 trees/hour safely
ranking.verdict: Best for truly automated extraction. Highest setup cost. ToS grey zone.

ranking.speed: fast but many endpoints return 403 for other users' trees
ranking.verdict: Useful for your own tree or DNA endpoints. Often blocked for others' tree data.
Always the first option to try. Ancestor can export GEDCOM from Ancestry via: Account > Trees > tree name > Tree Settings > Export tree.
